Unico IDtech — Bug Bounty
Submission Package
HackerOne Public Program | Security Assessment Report
Date: 2026-06-25 | Methodology: PTES-aligned, OWASP Top 10
Target
https://unico.io, *.unico.app, *.acesso.io, *.unico.run
Program
HackerOne — unico_idtech (Public)
Program URL
https://hackerone.com/unico_idtech
Scope
15 in-scope assets (see program info)
Test Date
2026-06-25
Assessor
Independent Security Researcher
Executive Summary
Unico IDtech (unico.io) is a Brazilian identity verification

ch (unico.io) is a Brazilian identity verification company with a public HackerOne
bug bounty program. The infrastructure spans Cloudflare (CDN/WAF), Google Cloud Platform
(hosting, Apigee API Gateway, Firebase), and multiple documentation platforms (GitBook,
Zendesk, Atlassian).
The assessment identified 6 findings including an OIDC provider misconfiguration (token
endpoint allowing unauthenticated requests), an Apigee proxy exposing internal architecture
via route failure errors, GCP project

architecture
via route failure errors, GCP project ID leakage in the Flutter web app, and several
information disclosure issues.
The primary focus of the Unico IDtech program is Liveness Bypass (up to $10,000), which
requires SDK-level testing not performed during this passive/active recon phase. Web
findings are secondary under the program structure.
Finding Summary
#
Finding
Severity
CVSS
Est. Bounty

OIDC token_endpoint allows "none" auth method

Total estimated bounty: $2,200–$7,900
Detailed Findings
1. OIDC Token Endpoint Allows "none" Auth Method
High
CVSS 7.5
Asset:

int Allows "none" Auth Method
High
CVSS 7.5
Asset: https://identityhomolog.acesso.io/.well-known/openid-configuration
Weakness: CWE-287: Improper Authentication (OIDC misconfiguration)
Description: The OpenID Connect provider exposes a discovery document where
token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported  includes "none"  — allowing OAuth2 token
requests without client authentication (no client_secret, no certificate).
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s

cret, no certificate).
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s "https://identityhomolog.acesso.io/.well-known/openid-configuration"
Evidence:
{
  "issuer": "https://identityhomolog.acesso.io",
  "token_endpoint": "https://identityhomolog.acesso.io/oauth2/token",
  "token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported": [
    "client_secret_basic",
    "client_secret_post",
    "none",
    "urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer"
  ],
  "grant_types_supported":

n-type:jwt-bearer"
  ],
  "grant_types_supported": 
["authorization_code","refresh_token","client_credentials","urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-
type:jwt-bearer"]
}
Impact:
Unauthenticated token requests possible if any client is configured to allow "none"
JWKS RSA public key exposed at /.well-known/jwks.json
Full OIDC endpoint disclosure enables targeted attacks
Capture File: recon/oidc_discovery.json
•
•
•
2. Apigee Proxy Route Failure — Internal Architecture
Leak
Medium
CVSS 5.3
Asset:

Internal Architecture
Leak
Medium
CVSS 5.3
Asset: https://backend-sdk.uat.private.unico.run/
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: All requests return HTTP 500 with Apigee error "Unable to route the
message to a Target Endpoint" . Confirms Google Apigee API Gateway with a broken
target endpoint.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -v "https://backend-sdk.uat.private.unico.run/"
Evidence:
HTTP/2 500
via: 1.1 google
{"fault":{"faultstring":"Unable to route the message to a

":{"faultstring":"Unable to route the message to a Target Endpoint","detail":
{"errorcode":"messaging.runtime.RouteFailed"}}}
Impact: Confirms Apigee API Gateway usage, reveals internal architecture, indicates
broken SDK backend.
Capture File: recon/apigee_error.json
3. GCP Project ID Exposed in Flutter JS Bundle
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://secure.unico.io/main.dart.js
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: The Flutter Web application (4.2MB JS bundle) contains the

ter Web application (4.2MB JS bundle) contains the Google Cloud
project ID acesso-unico-dev  and Firebase initialization code.
Steps to Reproduce:
grep -oP '[a-z][a-z0-9-]{3,29}' main.dart.js | sort -u | grep -i "acesso"
Evidence:
Project ID: acesso-unico-dev
Firebase DB: https://acesso-unico-dev.firebaseio.com/.json → HTTP 401 (project exists)
Impact: Enables targeted attacks against GCP resources, Firebase enumeration,
phishing.
4. OIDC Error Leaks Internal Validation (tenantId)
Low
CVSS

rror Leaks Internal Validation (tenantId)
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://identityhomolog.acesso.io/oauth2/token
Weakness: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
Description: Authorization_code grant with invalid code returns
{"error":"server_error","error_description":"requires property 'tenantId'"} , revealing
internal validation logic.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s -X POST -d 
"grant_type=authorization_code&code=test&client_id=test&redirect_uri=https://test.com"

st&client_id=test&redirect_uri=https://test.com"   
"https://identityhomolog.acesso.io/oauth2/token"
Evidence:
{"error":"server_error","error_description":"requires property 'tenantId'"}
Impact: Reveals multi-tenant architecture and parameter requirements.
5. IDP Session Cookie on Static Well-Known Endpoints
Low
CVSS 3.1
Asset: https://accountshomolog.acesso.io/
Weakness: CWE-384: Session Fixation
Description: Static endpoint redirects to /auth/idp  which sets a _session_idm  cookie
on every

th/idp  which sets a _session_idm  cookie
on every request — including unauthenticated requests to OIDC discovery endpoints.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -sv "https://accountshomolog.acesso.io/.well-known/openid-configuration" 2>&1 | grep -i 
"set-cookie"
Evidence:
set-cookie: _session_idm=s%3A...; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax
Impact: Session storage exhaustion, session fixation attack surface, technology stack
disclosure (Express/Node.js).
6. UAT Endpoints Blocked; Product

xpress/Node.js).
6. UAT Endpoints Blocked; Product Documentation
Exposed
Info
Assets: idpay-uat.unico.io , idcash-uat.unico.io , issuer.idpay-uat.unico.io ,
devcenter.unico.io
Description: UAT subdomains return Cloudflare 403 for all requests, making them
inaccessible. Devcenter documentation exposes product names (IDCloud, IDPay, People,
Sign, Auto, Identity) and their GitBook documentation sites.
Impact: Informational — blocked endpoints cannot be tested; documentation exposure
aids attack

nnot be tested; documentation exposure
aids attack surface mapping.

No data was exfiltrated, no services were disrupted, and no unauthorized access was obtained.

Vodafone Oman — Bug Bounty
Submission Package
HackerOne Private Program | Security Assessment Report
Date: 2026-06-25 | Methodology: PTES-aligned, OWASP Top 10
Target
https://www.vodafone.om/
Program
HackerOne — Vodafone Oman (Private)
Program URL
https://hackerone.com/vodafone_oman
Scope
*.vodafone.om
Test Date
2026-06-25
Assessor
Independent Security Researcher
Compliance
X-HackerOne-Research header + h1.researcher User-Agent used on all
requests
Executive Summary
Vodafone Oman's web

all
requests
Executive Summary
Vodafone Oman's web application (WordPress 6.2.2 on www.vodafone.om, React SPA on
business.vodafone.om) is protected by F5 BIG-IP ASM WAF on the main domain. The
assessment identified 11 findings including information disclosure, server-side request
forgery, WAF bypass vectors, outdated software with known CVEs, missing security headers,
and infrastructure hostname leaks.
The EventON plugin (version 4.4) has the most significant attack surface — it is

4) has the most significant attack surface — it is outdated
with 6+ unauthenticated CVEs, and its REST API at /api/eventon/v1/data  bypasses the WAF
entirely, accepting all EventON AJAX actions unauthenticated.
Finding Summary
#
Finding

Detailed Findings
1. Server Path Disclosure
Medium
CVSS 5.3
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/api/wp/v2/pages/14373
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: The WPBakery module shortcode leaks the absolute filesystem path /var/
www/vfo-corpp.zentity.com/public_html/  and internal hostname vfo-corpp.zentity.com .
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/api/wp/v2/pages/14373" | grep "Vodafone_Modules"
Response

v2/pages/14373" | grep "Vodafone_Modules"
Response excerpt:
[Vodafone_Modules module="/var/www/vfo-corpp.zentity.com/public_html/wp-content/plugins/vfo-wpbakery/lib/.
Impact:
Identifies OS and filesystem layout (Linux, /var/www/ structure)
Internal server hostname: vfo-corpp.zentity.com
Plugin path structure enables LFI/testing of custom modules
•
•
•
2. Yoast SEO SSRF via get_head Endpoint
Medium
CVSS 5.3
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/api/yoast/v1/get_head
Weakness: CWE-918: Server-Side

i/yoast/v1/get_head
Weakness: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
Description: Yoast SEO v20.10 exposes GET /api/yoast/v1/get_head?url=<URL>  without
authentication. The server fetches the provided URL, parses HTML for Yoast schema
metadata, and returns it. Confirmed outbound HTTP requests to arbitrary destinations
(response times ~1.3s confirm network round-trips).
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/api/yoast/v1/get_head?url=http://127.0.0.1/"
curl -s

i/yoast/v1/get_head?url=http://127.0.0.1/"
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/api/yoast/v1/get_head?url=http://vfo-corpp.zentity.com/"
Impact:
Blind SSRF to probe internal network services and ports
WAF behavioral reaction (blocked the endpoint after ~50 probes) confirms
severity
Potential access to cloud metadata endpoints if internal firewall permits
3. Development URL Leak (localhost:10019)
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/api/wp/v2/pages/15394
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of

i/wp/v2/pages/15394
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: Production WPBakery content includes a CSS background-image  URL
pointing to http://localhost:10019/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/banner.jpeg?id=9148 ,
revealing the development environment port and internal URL structure.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/api/wp/v2/pages/15394" | grep -o "localhost[^\"]*"
Impact: Reveals development infrastructure runs on port 10019, aids in mapping
internal

cture runs on port 10019, aids in mapping
internal architecture.
•
•
•
4. WAF Backup File Existence Disclosure
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/wp-config.save
Weakness: CWE-203: Observable Discrepancy
Description: The WAF returns HTTP 200 (block page) for sensitive paths that exist and
HTTP 404 for non-existent paths. Confirmed file existence: wp-config.save  (200) and
.wp-config.php.swp  (200). Non-existent paths like /readme.html  and /.env  return 404.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s

and /.env  return 404.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://www.vodafone.om/wp-config.save"
# → 200 (file exists, WAF blocking)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://www.vodafone.om/readme.html"
# → 404 (file does not exist)
Impact: Attackers can enumerate which sensitive files exist on the server for targeted
exploitation.
5. Outdated EventON 4.4 (Multiple CVEs)
Low
CVSS 3.7–7.5
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/  — EventON Premium plugin
Weakness:

w.vodafone.om/  — EventON Premium plugin
Weakness: CWE-1104: Use of Unmaintained Third-Party Components
Description: EventON Premium 4.4 (latest: 4.6.8+) with CVEs: CVE-2023-7200
(Reflected XSS), CVE-2024-0237 (Unauth Virtual Event Settings Update), ICS download
access, arbitrary metadata update, and stored XSS.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/" | grep "EventON"
# → <meta name="generator" content="EventON 4.4" />
curl -s -X POST -d

rator" content="EventON 4.4" />
curl -s -X POST -d "action=eventon_get_virtual_event&e_id=8708"   "https://www.vodafone.om/api/eventon/v1/d
# → {"r":{"action":"..."},"d":[]}
Impact: Unauthenticated attackers can exploit XSS, access private events, and modify
virtual event settings.
6. Missing Security Headers
Low
CVSS 3.1
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/
Weakness: CWE-693: Protection Mechanism Failure
Description: Missing HSTS, CSP, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy headers.
Present:

r-Policy, and Permissions-Policy headers.
Present: X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block.
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -sI "https://www.vodafone.om/" | grep -iE "(strict-transport|content-security|referrer|permissions)"
10. Internal Hostname via CORS Header
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://business.vodafone.om/
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: The business.vodafone.om  subdomain (no F5 WAF, nginx + React SPA)
returns access-control-allow-origin:

+ React SPA)
returns access-control-allow-origin: https://corpp-prod.ze.itcloud.vodafone.om .
Internal API path /api/backend/organizations/v1/simCards  found in JS bundle. GraphQL
endpoint at /api/graphql  returns 405 on POST (server is running, accepts different
methods).
Steps to Reproduce:
curl -sI "https://business.vodafone.om/" | grep -i "access-control"
# → access-control-allow-origin: https://corpp-prod.ze.itcloud.vodafone.om
11. EventON REST API WAF Bypass
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset:

1. EventON REST API WAF Bypass
Low
CVSS 3.7
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/api/eventon/v1/data
Weakness: CWE-862: Missing Authorization
Description: While admin-ajax.php  is WAF-blocked, the EventON REST API at /api/
eventon/v1/data  accepts all EventON AJAX actions unauthenticated. All 11 tested actions
are routed to the EventON handler. Write actions require nonce (returns 403), but read
actions are fully accessible. Works with all Content-Type headers.
Steps to Reproduce:
# Direct AJAX (WAF

pe headers.
Steps to Reproduce:
# Direct AJAX (WAF blocked):
curl -s "https://www.vodafone.om/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php?action=eventon_get_virtual_event"
# → "Request Rejected"
# Via REST API (bypasses WAF):
curl -s -X POST -d "action=eventon_get_virtual_event&e_id=8708"   "https://www.vodafone.om/api/eventon/v1/d
# → {"r":{"action":"eventon_get_virtual_event","e_id":"8708"},"d":[]}
Informational / Supporting Findings
7. No Rate Limiting on REST API
Info
Asset:

ndings
7. No Rate Limiting on REST API
Info
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/api/
Description: 10 consecutive requests returned HTTP 200 with no throttling. Enables
brute-force and data scraping.
8. Author ID Enumeration
Info
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/  — Yoast SEO schema
Description: Author IDs 12, 13, 14, 43, 44 discovered via Yoast JSON-LD schema. User
enumeration via /api/wp/v2/users  is properly blocked.
9. WAF Dynamic IP Blocking
Info
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/  — F5 BIG-IP

Info
Asset: https://www.vodafone.om/  — F5 BIG-IP ASM
Description: After ~50 Yoast SSRF probes, WAF escalated from endpoint blocking to
full IP block. Returns HTTP 200 (not 429) for blocked requests. Support IDs observed:
15156904961780786245 , 15156904961781454318 , 15156904961781469014 ,
15156904961781389783 .

ALSCO — Bug Bounty Submission
Package
HackerOne Public Program | Security Assessment Report
Date: 2026-06-25 | Methodology: PTES-aligned, OWASP Top 10
Target
sandbox.securegateway.com, sandbox-royal.securegateway.com
Program

Executive Summary
ALSCO is a security technology company behind Secure Gateway, a multi-patented nginx-
based

hind Secure Gateway, a multi-patented nginx-
based security module. Their bug bounty program on HackerOne covers two sandbox
environments.
The assessment identified 5 findings, primarily related to infrastructure exposure and
configuration issues. The sandbox servers are heavily locked down by the Secure Gateway
product itself — all requests return a generic 302 redirect to an error page, making deep
application-layer testing impossible without a pre-configured test account.
The program's

thout a pre-configured test account.
The program's primary focus area (Secure Gateway bypass) cannot be fully tested as the
required validation site checksw.com  does not resolve.

Detailed Findings
1. Origin Server IP Exposure — Cloudflare Bypass
Medium
CVSS 5.3
Asset: sandbox.securegateway.com  → 188.34.187.32
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: Both in-scope sandboxes resolve to 188.34.187.32  — a dedicated Hetzner
origin IP not behind Cloudflare. All other securegateway.com subdomains use
Cloudflare proxy IPs

uregateway.com subdomains use
Cloudflare proxy IPs (188.114.x.x). This bypasses all Cloudflare security layers: WAF
rules, DDoS protection, IP reputation, bot management, and rate limiting.
Evidence:
nginx version disclosed: HTTP port 80 returns Server: nginx/1.26.1
Wildcard SSL cert exposed: *.securegateway.com  via ZeroSSL (vs Google Trust
on CF)
Hosting identified: Hetzner Online GmbH, Falkenstein, Germany (AS24940)
Ports open: 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS) confirmed on origin
Host header

HTTP), 443 (HTTPS) confirmed on origin
Host header validation: Only sandbox hostnames accepted; others trigger
connection reset
PoC:
# 1. DNS shows direct IP (no Cloudflare)
dig +short A sandbox.securegateway.com   # 188.34.187.32
dig +short A www.securegateway.com       # 188.114.96.12 (Cloudflare)
# 2. Direct IP access
curl -sk -H "Host: sandbox.securegateway.com" "https://188.34.187.32/"
# 3. nginx version leaked
curl -sI "http://188.34.187.32/" | grep Server  # nginx/1.26.1
# 4. Wildcard

7.32/" | grep Server  # nginx/1.26.1
# 4. Wildcard SSL cert exposed
echo | openssl s_client -connect 188.34.187.32:443   -servername sandbox.securegateway.com 2>/
dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer
# 5. Hosting info
curl -s "https://ipinfo.io/188.34.187.32/json" | grep -E "org|hostname|city|country"
Impact: WAF bypass, DDoS exposure, infrastructure leak (Hetzner DE, nginx 1.26.1,
ZeroSSL wildcard cert).
Remediation: Enable Cloudflare proxy (orange cloud) on sandbox DNS; restrict

lare proxy (orange cloud) on sandbox DNS; restrict origin
firewall to CF IP ranges; disable HTTP port 80; strip nginx version.

2. HTTPS to HTTP Downgrade on Error Redirects
Low
CVSS 3.1
Asset: www.securegateway.com  → msg.securegateway.com
Weakness: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
Description: The redirect chain starts on HTTPS but downgrades to HTTP: https://
www.securegateway.com/ → https://msg.securegateway.com/msg/ →

ateway.com/ → https://msg.securegateway.com/msg/ → http://
msg.securegateway.com/msg/sg_err.php?...
Evidence:
HTTP/2 307
location: https://msg.securegateway.com/msg/
HTTP/2 302
location: http://msg.securegateway.com/msg/sg_err.php?err=22&SG_ID=...
Impact: SG_ID transmitted in cleartext; error page subject to MITM tampering; phishing
attack surface.
Remediation: Use HTTPS exclusively in all redirect Location headers.
3. X-Secure_Gateway_ID Header — Unique Request
Tracking
Low
CVSS 3.1
Asset:

ader — Unique Request
Tracking
Low
CVSS 3.1
Asset: sandbox.securegateway.com
Weakness: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information
Description: Every response includes a unique 32-char hex X-Secure_Gateway_ID
header that changes per request, enabling request tracking and correlation.
Evidence:
X-Secure_Gateway_ID: c1dc912d6c0a20fe2e9471260cccc80e
Impact: Request fingerprinting; potential correlation with server-side logs; information
disclosure.
Remediation: Remove or restrict the header to

ure.
Remediation: Remove or restrict the header to debugging environments only.
4. Bypass Test Site (checksw.com) Unresolvable
Info
Asset: http://checksw.com
Description: The program policy requires bypass reports to be reproducible on
checksw.com , but this domain returns NXDOMAIN. Researchers cannot validate bypass
findings per policy requirements.
Evidence:
dig +short A checksw.com
# (no output - NXDOMAIN)
Impact: Blocks the program's primary attack vector (Secure Gateway bypass) from
being

y attack vector (Secure Gateway bypass) from
being tested or submitted.
5. cPanel Auto-Generated Subdomains Exposed
Info
Asset: alscotoday.com
Description: Extensive cPanel auto-generated subdomains publicly resolvable
(cpanel.*, webdisk.*, webmail.*, cpcalendars.*). Third-party subdomain
buckertlawfirm.alscotoday.com  also exposed.
Impact: Reveals cPanel hosting; increases attack surface; potential phishing vector via
third-party subdomain.
